text.title: Creative Brief Template Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding the creative brief. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: One brief MUST cover exactly one paid-ads campaign instance. Multi-campaign briefs are forbidden — split into one per campaign with explicit cross-links.
rule.rationale: Combined briefs lose the rationale per variant; downstream designers cannot tell which input justified which creative.

rule.statement: Every input field MUST have a declared type AND cite a source (URL, doc path, ticket ID). Free-form prose without a citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped + unsourced inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values; typed + sourced inputs make the brief falsifiable.

rule.statement: The brief MUST name a single accountable owner (handle, email, or rotating role with handle). "Team", "we", or empty is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Future readers need a named human to chase when the brief is stale.

rule.statement: The brief MUST carry a semver-style version and a last_reviewed ISO date. Briefs >30 days old MUST be flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Briefs decay as audiences + ad platforms change; freshness gating forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Every creative decision (angle, hook, CTA) MUST cite at least one input field by name. Decisions without traces are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without traceability, the brief becomes opinion. Citations make the brief reviewable by a future operator with no context.
